data.title: Solana Policy Institute urges SEC to exempt DeFi developers from exchange rules
data.description:
The Solana Policy Institute urged the SEC to distinguish non-custodial DeFi code from exchanges, warning current rules could chill innovation.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/solana-policy-institute-sec-defi-developer-protections?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Solana Policy Institute Urges SEC to Differentiate Between Centralized and Decentralized Crypto Platforms
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the Solana Policy Institute, a nonprofit organization focused on blockchain policy, has called on the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to make a clear distinction between centralized cryptocurrency exchanges and non-custodial decentralized finance (DeFi) software. The institute argues that developers of DeFi applications should not be regulated as intermediaries, emphasizing that creating and publishing non-custodial code is fundamentally different from controlling or intermediating the underlying funds.

The institute's letter, sent on Friday, highlights the inappropriateness of applying Exchange Act 3b-16 to developers of non-custodial protocols. This act is typically relevant to exchange operators who custody assets, control execution flow, and act as intermediaries. The letter states, "Transactions that take place via a smart contract protocol are not the regulatory equivalent of trading on an exchange or ATS and should not be treated as such." The Solana Policy Institute is urging the SEC to provide guidance on differentiating between non-custodial software tools and exchanges with brokers. Additionally, the institute recommends amending Act 3b-16 to exclude open-source code from the "exchange" definition and adopting a custody-and-control-based framework to distinguish between intermediated and disintermediated blockchain activities.

The letter further warns that treating DeFi code in the same manner as centralized trading platforms could discourage innovation and push activities offshore to unregulated channels, thereby reducing the competitiveness of the United States. To safeguard DeFi developers and maintain onshore activity, the SEC should establish "clear, durable lines between software tools and actual intermediaries that exercise custody, discretion, or control over funds or transactions," the letter suggests. The issue of developer liability has gained significant attention in recent years, especially following criminal cases involving developers of non-custodial protocols, such as Tornado Cash co-founders Roman Storm and Alexey Pertsev, who were found guilty of operating an unlicensed money-transmitting business despite their protocol being non-custodial and never controlling user funds.

In a related development, U.S. Senators Cynthia Lummis and Ron Wyden have introduced legislation aimed at protecting blockchain developers who do not directly handle user funds, exempting them from money transmitter regulations. The Blockchain Regulatory Certainty Act seeks to clarify that writing software or maintaining networks should not trigger federal or state money-transfer requirements, which have been a growing concern for developers. "Blockchain developers who have simply written code and maintain open-source infrastructure have lived under threat of being classified as money transmitters for far too long," Lummis stated, adding that the bill aims to provide developers with more clarity for building the "future of digital finance without fear of prosecution." Meanwhile, the US Senate Agriculture Committee has postponed its markup of the crypto market structure bill until late January, with Chairman John Boozman noting the need for additional time to secure broader bipartisan support. Boozman emphasized that advancing a bill with cross-party backing remains the priority, citing "meaningful progress" and "constructive discussions" as part of the ongoing efforts.

data.title: A Polymarket trader lost $2.36 million in 8 days, with a win rate of 47.2%.
data.description: Lookonchain reported on its X platform that a Polymarket trader lost $2.36 million in eight days. This trader made 53 predictions over the past eight days, with 25 profitable and 28 losing, resulting in a win rate of 47.2%. Their trading was primarily concentrated in the sports market, with winning trades typically yielding returns between 60% and 150%, while losing trades resulted in the loss of all their capital.

data.title: Polymarket drove Polygon's fee revenue to over $1.7 million, and burned over 12.5 million POL.
data.description: Castle Labs stated on the X platform that Polygon has generated over $1.7 million in protocol fee revenue since the beginning of the year and has burned over 12.5 million POL tokens, worth over $1.5 million. The main reason for the revenue increase is Polymarket's introduction of fees for its 15-minute prediction market. In the past 24 hours, Polymarket contributed over $100,000 in fees to Polygon.

data.title: Golden Globes and Polymarket Prediction Season Coincide
data.description: According to Odaily, the official Golden Globes announcement highlighted the overlap between the awards season and Polymarket's prediction season. The statement was accompanied by an image of the Polymarket Golden Globes prediction market dashboard.

data.title: Polymarket Trader Faces Significant Losses Over Eight Days
data.description: According to Odaily, a trader on the Polymarket platform has incurred losses totaling $2.36 million over the past eight days. The trader executed 53 predictions during this period, achieving profits in 25 instances and losses in 28, resulting in a success rate of 47.2%. The majority of the trader's activities were focused on sports markets, where profitable trades yielded returns ranging from 60% to 150%, while losing trades resulted in the loss of the entire principal.

data.title: Strategy Board member Carl Rickertsen purchased 5,000 shares of MSTR for $779,000.
data.description: BitcoinTreasuries.NET reported on the X platform that Strategy Board member Carl Rickertsen purchased 5,000 shares of MSTR at $155.88 each, for a total of $779,000. This transaction was seen as a bargain purchase by Carl Rickertsen.

data.title: Maitong MSX adjusts RWA spot rates to "one-way fee," with zero commission on sales.
data.description: US-based cryptocurrency exchange MSX (msx.com) announced today a change to its RWA spot trading fee structure, effective immediately. The new fee structure changes the previous "two-way fee" to a "one-way fee." Specifically, the fee for buying remains at 0.3%, while the fee for selling is reduced to 0%. This means that users will experience a substantial 50% reduction in overall transaction costs when completing a full "buy + sell" trading cycle. This fee policy is now in effect across the entire MSX platform, covering all listed RWA spot trading pairs.

data.title: Polymarket launched its Golden Globe Awards prediction market dashboard at the Golden Globe Awards ceremony.
data.description: The Golden Globes officially posted: "Golden Globes season meets Polymarket's prediction season," accompanied by a picture of Polymarket's Golden Globes prediction market dashboard.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Sells Tokens for Ethereum
data.description: According to Foresight News, Lookonchain reported that Vitalik.eth has sold some tokens he received for free from his wallet in the past 30 minutes, acquiring 9.4 ETH.

data.title: U.S. December CPI Report Anticipated Amid Market Uncertainty
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, the U.S. December Consumer Price Index (CPI) is set to be released tonight at 21:30 UTC+8. Following a surprising decline in November, the market anticipates a slight increase in December's CPI. However, there may be potential 'data traps' within the report. Recent non-farm payroll data has nearly extinguished hopes for an interest rate cut in January. Additionally, U.S. President Donald Trump faces an unexpected investigation, adding to market uncertainty. Investors are advised to remain cautious as the report could further influence market dynamics.

data.title: Strategy Board Member Acquires MSTR Shares
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Carl Rickertsen, a member of the Strategy Board, has purchased 5,000 shares of MSTR stock. The transaction was executed at a price of $155.88 per share, amounting to a total of $779,395.

data.title: Crypto Custody Provider BitGo Files for $200 Million U.S. Listing
data.description:
Palo Alto firm is the latest digital asset company to pursue public markets as it seeks up to $1. 96 billion valuation.
source: https://www.blockhead.co/2026/01/13/crypto-custody-provider-bitgo-files-for-200-million-u-s-listing/

data.title: Gate will launch Fogo (FOGO).
data.description: According to the official announcement, Gate will launch spot trading of Fogo (FOGO) on January 15th at 22:00 (UTC+8). Fogo (FOGO) will also be listed on the Flash Exchange platform at 23:00 (UTC+8).

data.title: Senate unveils updated market structure bill limiting stablecoin rewards on idle holdings
data.description:
The bill seeks to bar digital asset providers from paying interest just for holding a stablecoin, but would allow activity-based rewards.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/385303/senate-unveils-new-draft-bill-stablecoin-reward?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: VanEck Says 2026 Will Be Risk-On Quarter Despite Bitcoin Cycle Break
data.description:
VanEck positions 2026 as a risk-on year for investors, highlighting AI valuations reset after 50% corrections, private credit yields at 9%, and gold's structural re-emergence despite Bitcoin breaking its four-year cycle and facing near-term caution.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/vaneck-says-2026-will-be-risk-on-quarter-despite-bitcoin-cycle-break/

data.title: Web3 Projects Urged to Improve Transparency on RootData
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, Web3 asset data platform RootData has issued a daily transparency alert on Twitter (X), highlighting several projects, including Only Layer, LITAS.IO, Reltime, Lithosphere, and APX Lending, for missing core information. RootData is urging these projects to submit or update their details on the platform to enhance their transparency scores. Additionally, RootData has committed to continuously monitoring and exposing 'black box' projects that lack essential information.

RootData's transparency score is designed to assess the completeness and timeliness of a project's information disclosure. The score ranges from A to F, with lower scores indicating less comprehensive information disclosure and higher risks of misconduct. Investors are advised to exercise caution with projects that have lower transparency scores.

data.title: Global Central Banks to Issue Statement Supporting Fed Chair Powell
data.description: According to PANews, multiple central banks worldwide are drafting a statement to express their support for U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. This joint statement is expected to be released under the name of the Bank for International Settlements (BIS).

data.title: Bitmine Amasses 4.17M ETH as Crypto and Cash Holdings Hit $14B
data.description:
Bitmine has further cemented its position as the largest corporate holder of Ether after adding more than 24,000 ETH to its balance sheet over the past week.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitmine-amasses-4-17m-eth-as-crypto-and-cash-holdings-hit-14b/
